fullText: 19 new features 31 bug fixes and 20 improvements across two releases Starting with Windows PowerShell is now available as an opt-in shell tool So Windows users finally have a native alternative to Bash on the hook side for upgrades A new task created hook fires when tasks are created Workry create hooks can return the workree path over HTTP Hooks now support a conditional if field using permission rule syntax so they only fire when they actually need to The MCP system gets a major upgrade Servers configured both locally and through clot.ai connectors are now dduplicated Local config wins Tool descriptions are kept at 2 kilobytes to prevent open API servers from bloating your context Oof follows RFC FC9728 for authorization discovery and enterprise admins get a new allowed channel plug-in setting to lock down what teams can install Five plug-in bug fixes ship alongside including step-up off and blocked server enforcement Six quality of life improvements If you return after 75 minutes idle cla code nudges you to clear saving you from reaching a stale session Deep links open in your preferred terminal and now support up to 5,000 characters Token counts above 1 million display as 1.5 m instead of 15 12.6K and startup is around 30 milliseconds faster BS code gets a rate limit warning banner showing your usage percentage and when it resets The stat screenshot is 16 times faster and works in all builds Now under the hood the layout engine swapped from wasome yoga layout to pure Typescript which makes scrolling through large transcripts noticeably smoother Unavailable/comands like /voice and /mo are now hidden instead of showing errors Voice push to talk no longer leaks characters into your text input ControlU properly clears across line boundaries in multi-line mode Null unbinding keybindings actually freeze the prefix key Now for international users native terminal cursor tracking works with IME composition So CJK input renders inline and after exiting CL code C and control D work correctly in Ghosty Kitty and Westerm A new environment variable lets you configure the streaming idle timeout Default is 90 seconds Every API request now includes an Xclient request ID header for debugging timeouts System prompt caching works globally even with tool search enabled Persistent ECON reset errors during edge connection churn are fixed with fresh TCP connections on retry and a memory leak in remote sessions is patched Seven stability fixes /compact no longer fails when your conversation is too large for the compact request itself to fit Mac OS keychain errors that caused Spirious not locked in messages are resolved The cold start race condition where core tools were deferred without bypass is fixed Partial clone repositories no longer trigger mass blob downloads on startup and remote control sessions no longer get stuck on requires action Run clot update to get both releases right now Over 75,000 developers are already building with cloud code on GitHub If these updates help your workflow subscribe and hit the bell PowerShell or Bash What are you running cloud code with Tell me below
